_note: Four CV angles. cv_agent.py picks the right one based on JD role_type.

rust_mcp.title: Rust Systems Engineer — AI Agent Infrastructure
rust_mcp.summary: I build the performance layer for AI agents. Most agent infrastructure runs on Python — GC pauses, slow serialization, 50-200ms overhead per tool call. At 1000 tool calls in an agentic loop, that's 3 minutes of dead time. I build in Rust: zero-cost abstractions, no garbage collector, deterministic memory. Production MCP server. 11 live tools. Zero runtime surprises.

positioning.wedge: Python MCP = slow. Rust MCP = fast. Most teams haven't made this switch yet. I already shipped it.
positioning.machine_statement: I don't add features until the system is fast. Speed is the feature.

skills_priority: Rust (tokio, rmcp, serde, sqlx, reqwest)
MCP Protocol (Model Context Protocol) — JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio
Async systems design (multi-threaded, zero-GC)
Claude API — claude-opus-4-8, adaptive thinking, tool use
Python (asyncio, aiohttp, FastAPI)
SQLite / PostgreSQL
Docker
experience_bullets: Architected production rmcp MCP server in Rust — 11 live tools: GitHub user/org search, YC company API, Hunter.io email discovery, WebReveal tech stack detection, RemoteOK job scraping, SQLite prospect pipeline

Built compliance layer on top of MCP server: sliding-window rate limiter (tokio::sync::Mutex + VecDeque<Instant>), PII detection via compiled Regex (OnceLock), per-call audit log with output fingerprinting

Built multi-agent Python pipeline (CV Agent + Outreach Agent + Research Agent + Classifier Agent) using claude-opus-4-8 with asyncio.gather() — 3 parallel Claude calls per company, controlled concurrency via asyncio.Semaphore
Integrated Claude Vision API (claude-opus-4-8) for paper scan detection: MCQ tick mark detection + handwriting OCR across 11 answer parameters per sheet

opening_lines.default: I build fast AI agent infrastructure in Rust — because at agent scale, every millisecond compounds and Python's garbage collector doesn't care about your SLA.
opening_lines.rust_company: Rust engineer focused on zero-latency AI agent tooling. Production MCP server, 11 tools, tokio async, zero GC pauses.
opening_lines.ai_company: I ship the performance layer that makes AI agents fast. Most teams run Python MCP servers. Mine runs in Rust — deterministic, zero-overhead, production-ready.
opening_lines.infra_company: Systems engineer who crossed over into AI infrastructure. I build MCP servers in Rust because the protocol deserves a runtime that won't pause mid-tool-call.
opening_lines.protocol_company: Protocol engineer who ships in Rust. Built production MCP server (JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio), rate limiter, PII filter, audit layer — the full compliance stack for agent tool use.

data_engineering.title: AI & Data Engineer — LLM Automation + Full-Stack Systems
data_engineering.summary: I build AI-powered data systems that replace manual workflows. PostgreSQL pipelines, Power BI dashboards, LLM-driven automation across 15+ integrated APIs. Most data work stays trapped in Excel — I pull it into automated pipelines with AI analysis at the output layer.

positioning.wedge: Data work that used to require 3 analysts now runs on one automated pipeline. I build the pipeline.
positioning.machine_statement: Manual data workflows are just automation waiting to happen.

skills_priority: Python (pandas, asyncio, FastAPI, requests)
PostgreSQL / SQLite — query optimization, schema design, CRUD pipelines
Power BI — dashboard design, DAX, data modeling
Claude API — claude-opus-4-8, document analysis, structured extraction
REST API integration (15+ APIs: Hunter.io, WebReveal, RemoteOK, GitHub, YC, Companies House)
Node.js / Express / MongoDB
React / TypeScript
Docker
experience_bullets: Designed and shipped PostgreSQL data warehouse from zero across 15-location edtech company: 30+ normalized tables (HRMS, KPI, EdTech schemas) — replaced 15 branch spreadsheets with single source of truth; 95% data accuracy, 85% branch reporting overhead eliminated

Built real-time 40+ KPI monitoring matrix: revenue/branch, enrollment funnel, seat utilization, teacher hours, lead→demo→enrollment ratio — Power BI dashboards with RAG alerting for daily founder visibility across 50+ staff

Architected 15+ API integration fabric: Zoho CRM/Books, Google API Console, HRMS, EdTech DB, LLM providers, SMS/email dispatch — zero manual handoffs, fully automated lead capture → routing → follow-up → outcome → KPI pipeline

Shipped OCR + handwriting recognition pipeline (Test Portal): scanned answer sheets → text classification → auto-graded scores → KPI DB in minutes; Claude Vision API for MCQ tick mark detection across 11 parameters per sheet
Integrated 15+ external APIs into networking agent data pipeline: GitHub REST v3, YC API, Hunter.io, WebReveal, RemoteOK, Companies House — normalized output into SQLite with enrichment layer

opening_lines.default: I build AI systems that automate data workflows — from API integration pipelines to LLM-powered analysis layers that turn raw data into structured, actionable output.
opening_lines.data_company: Data engineer who builds AI automation on top of existing data stacks. PostgreSQL, Power BI, 15 API integrations, Claude-powered analysis — all production.
opening_lines.ai_company: I use Claude API to build real automation: document extraction, structured data analysis, multi-agent pipelines. Not demos — deployed systems replacing manual workflows.
opening_lines.fullstack_company: Full-stack engineer with a data and AI automation focus. React + Node.js frontend, PostgreSQL/MongoDB backend, Claude API integration for intelligent data processing.

ai_ml_infra.title: AI Infrastructure Engineer — LLM Pipelines, Multi-Agent Systems, Automation
ai_ml_infra.summary: I build production AI systems — not demos. Multi-model LLM pipeline (Ollama local inference + cloud APIs + Vector DB) running across 50+ staff: staff logs → LLM → personalized output → dispatch → outcome logged → KPI updated. 80% reduction in manual communication prep. Parallel multi-agent Python orchestration with Claude. OCR + handwriting classification. Rust MCP server as zero-latency tool-use layer.

positioning.wedge: I don't fine-tune models — I build the infrastructure that makes LLMs useful in real workflows: the pipeline, dispatch, feedback loop, monitoring. End to end.
positioning.machine_statement: An LLM demo and a production LLM system are two different things. I build the second kind.

skills_priority: Claude API — claude-opus-4-8, adaptive thinking, Vision, streaming, tool use
Ollama — local inference, model routing, multi-model selection
Multi-agent orchestration — asyncio.gather(), parallel Claude calls, Semaphore concurrency control
Vector DB + embedding pipelines + RAG system design
Python (asyncio, aiohttp, FastAPI) — LLM pipeline orchestration
OCR + document intelligence (Claude Vision API, text classification)
Rust (tokio, rmcp) — MCP server, zero-latency tool dispatch layer
PostgreSQL / SQLite / Power BI — output → KPI pipeline
experience_bullets: Built multi-model LLM system in production across 50+ staff: Ollama local inference + cloud APIs + Vector DB; pipeline — staff performance logs + lead data → LLM → personalized call scripts, email sequences, performance summaries → SMTP dispatch → outcome logged → KPI updated; 80% reduction in manual communication prep
Built LLM employee efficiency monitoring: tracks output quality per staff member, surfaces underperformance alerts in real-time to Power BI dashboard — measurable vs zero baseline 12 months prior

Built parallel multi-agent Python orchestration using claude-opus-4-8 with adaptive thinking: 3 specialized Claude agents per company simultaneously (Tech Classifier, AI Hunger Detector, Proposal Generator); asyncio.gather() + asyncio.Semaphore(N) — 9 parallel Claude calls, 10 companies analyzed in ~60s

Integrated Claude Vision API for document intelligence: POST endpoint accepts scanned answer sheets, extracts MCQ tick marks + handwritten answers with confidence scoring across 11 parameters per sheet; minutes vs hours manually
Architected Rust MCP server (rmcp, tokio) as zero-latency tool-use layer for Claude agents: 11 live tools, zero GC pauses, stdio transport, compliance layer (rate limiter, PII redaction, audit log)

opening_lines.default: I build production LLM pipelines — not experiments. Multi-model system, parallel agent orchestration, automated dispatch loops, real-time outcome tracking. Running in production across 50+ staff.
opening_lines.llm_company: I run multi-model LLM pipelines in production: Ollama local + cloud APIs + Vector DB, parallel Claude agents with adaptive thinking, automated output→dispatch→KPI feedback loops. Not a demo.
opening_lines.agent_company: Multi-agent systems in production. Parallel async Claude agents, asyncio.gather() concurrency control, automated output dispatch with outcome tracking. Rust MCP server as the tool layer.
opening_lines.mlops_company: I build the infrastructure layer for LLMs in real workflows: pipeline orchestration, model routing, output dispatch, monitoring. The part most ML teams skip.

protocol_engineer.title: Protocol & Systems Engineer — Rust + Distributed Infra
protocol_engineer.summary: I design and implement communication protocols in Rust. Production MCP server (JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio), async transport design, compliance-grade audit layer. I work at the protocol level — where wire format decisions, serialization choices, and runtime behavior determine system reliability.

positioning.wedge: Most engineers use protocols. I build them. MCP, stdio transport, rate-limited async dispatch — shipped in Rust.
positioning.machine_statement: The protocol layer is where correctness matters most. Get it wrong there and nothing above it works.

skills_priority: Rust (tokio async runtime, serde, reqwest, sqlx, rmcp)
Protocol design — MCP (JSON-RPC 2.0), stdio/HTTP transport layers
Async systems — multi-threaded tokio, zero-copy deserialization
Distributed systems patterns — rate limiting, audit logging, idempotency
API design (REST, WebSocket, stdio)
Python (asyncio, aiohttp, FastAPI)
SQLite / PostgreSQL
Docker / Linux
experience_bullets: Built DVT-FOCIL integration library (Rust): enables DVT clusters (SSV Network, Obol Charon, Anchor) to participate in FOCIL inclusion list committees — censorship resistance for 15-25% of Ethereum stake running on distributed validators; addresses single-signer assumption gap in EIP-7805 spec

Implemented EIP-7805 FOCIL (Fork-Choice Enforced Inclusion Lists) in Rust targeting Ethereum Heze fork: 16-validator IL committee, fork-choice enforcement, censorship-resistant transaction inclusion — based on ethereum/consensus-specs

Built p2pflow: eBPF application for passive Ethereum p2p network monitoring — TCP flow tracking + RTT probing via kernel programs (Aya framework, Linux ≥5.8), reads perf event maps without modifying Geth/Lighthouse node processes

Built protocol-engine-core: multi-crate Rust workspace — HotStuff 2-chain BFT consensus with full view-change/timeout path (NewViewMessage → NewViewCollector → leader rotation), QC threshold fires exactly once at f+1 boundary; 8-test view-change suite, 6 fault injection tests

Designed EIP-1559 priority mempool with bounded eviction: BTreeMap ordered by (priority_fee DESC, hash), O(1) pop_last on overflow; 5-test eviction suite + proptest fuzzing — eliminates OOM risk on long mempool runs
Implemented Ethereum Engine API (forkchoice_updated + PayloadAttributes → payload_id → get_payload round-trip): 14-test suite covering timestamp/randao, reorg detection, sync state transitions

Designed and shipped production MCP server in Rust: JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, 11 tool handlers, rmcp macro-based dispatch, tokio multi-thread runtime; compliance layer: sliding-window rate limiter, PII detection via OnceLock Regex, per-call audit log with output fingerprinting

opening_lines.default: Protocol engineer who ships in Rust. I work at the wire level — transport design, serialization, async dispatch, rate limiting. Production MCP server live.
opening_lines.infra_company: Systems engineer with production Rust protocol work. Built MCP server, sliding-window rate limiter, PII pipeline — the full compliance stack for agent tool use at scale.
opening_lines.distributed_company: I build distributed systems infrastructure in Rust: async transport, protocol dispatch, rate limiters, audit logs. Shipped in production. No runtime surprises.
opening_lines.p2p_company: Rust systems engineer with deep protocol experience. Built production MCP (JSON-RPC 2.0) server, custom rate limiter, async HTTP pipeline — comfortable at the transport and serialization layer.

projects.name: Networking Agent — Production Rust MCP Server
projects.description: MCP server with 11 tools: GitHub user/org search, YC company data, tech stack detection (WebReveal), email finding (Hunter.io), remote job search (RemoteOK), SQLite prospect pipeline. Multi-agent Python layer: Research Agent, Classifier Agent, CV Agent, Outreach Agent — all parallel async Claude calls.
projects.signal: One of the first production Rust MCP servers publicly available. MCP is the protocol Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and OpenAI are standardizing on for agent tool use.

projects.name: Student Assessment Portal — AI Paper Scanning
projects.description: Full-stack assessment system with AI scan detection. POST /api/scan-paper sends scanned sheets to Claude Vision — MCQ tick mark detection + handwriting OCR across 11 parameters, confidence scoring, structured JSON output. Replaces manual marking workflow.
projects.signal: Production AI system replacing manual assessment workflow. Demonstrates Claude Vision API for document analysis at scale.

projects.name: DVT-FOCIL Integration Library
projects.description: Distributed Validator Technology integration for FOCIL (EIP-7805). Enables DVT clusters to participate in Fork-Choice Enforced Inclusion List committees — censorship resistance for 15-25% of Ethereum stake on distributed validators. Addresses the single-signer assumption gap in the base EIP-7805 spec.
projects.signal: Ethereum consensus layer R&D — original contribution to censorship resistance infrastructure.

projects.name: FOCIL — EIP-7805 Rust Implementation
projects.description: Rust implementation of EIP-7805 Fork-Choice Enforced Inclusion Lists targeting the Ethereum Heze fork. 16-validator IL committee, fork-choice enforcement rule, censorship-resistant transaction inclusion layer. Based on ethereum/consensus-specs.
projects.signal: Implementing live Ethereum EIPs in production Rust — consensus layer engineering.

projects.name: p2pflow — eBPF Ethereum P2P Monitor
projects.description: eBPF application for passive Ethereum p2p network traffic monitoring. TCP flow tracking and RTT probing via kernel programs (Aya framework, Linux ≥5.8). Reads perf event maps in userspace — monitors Geth/Lighthouse p2p traffic without modifying node processes.
projects.signal: Kernel-level Ethereum network observability — rare combination of eBPF + Ethereum protocol knowledge.

projects.name: Axiom Engine — ZK-Proof AI Agent Engine
projects.description: Proof-native AI agent engine in Rust. Every answer comes with mathematical proof of correctness, cryptographic execution integrity, and ZK-verifiable computation. Solves AI hallucination at the verification layer — not prompt engineering.
projects.signal: ZK + AI agent intersection — frontier research area combining formal verification with LLM outputs.

projects.name: AquafySystems — IoT/AI Water Treatment
projects.description: Affordable decentralized greywater treatment using IoT and AI. Founded 2024, Bhubaneswar. Targets water management for 600M people in water-scarce areas. Government of India recognized.
projects.signal: SIH 2023 Runner-up (500+ teams). Government of India recognition. Real-world IoT/AI deployment.

rust_mcp.performance: Python MCP servers add 50-200ms per tool call. Rust doesn't. At agent scale, that gap is your product's speed. I already built and shipped the Rust version.
rust_mcp.rare_combo: Most AI engineers can't write systems code. Most systems engineers don't understand LLM tool use. I work at both layers simultaneously.
rust_mcp.shipped: Not a side project. Production rmcp server, 11 live tools, stdio transport, async tokio runtime, SQLite persistence. Running now.
rust_mcp.timing: MCP is 6 months old. The teams that build the fastest MCP infrastructure in the next 12 months will own the agent tooling space. I started 6 months ago.

data_engineering.automation: Manual data workflows are just automation waiting to happen. I replaced analyst hours with Python + Claude pipelines across 15 API integrations at a 15-location company.
data_engineering.ai_layer: I don't just build data pipelines — I put Claude at the output layer. Structured extraction, automated analysis, document processing. The pipeline thinks.
data_engineering.fullstack: Full-stack + data + AI automation in one. React frontend, Python/Node backend, PostgreSQL, Power BI dashboards, Claude integration — no handoffs needed.
data_engineering.scale: Built data infrastructure for 50+ staff across 15 locations. 30+ table PostgreSQL warehouse, 40+ KPI Power BI dashboards, 15 API integrations. All production.

ai_ml_infra.production: I don't build LLM demos. I build the pipeline that runs daily across 50+ staff: logs → LLM → personalized output → dispatch → outcome tracked → KPI updated. 80% reduction in manual prep.
ai_ml_infra.multi_model: Multi-model in production: Ollama local inference + cloud APIs + Vector DB. Model routing based on task type, output quality monitoring, automated re-dispatch on low confidence.
ai_ml_infra.multi_agent: 3 parallel Claude agents per company, asyncio.gather() + Semaphore concurrency control, 9 simultaneous LLM calls, 10 companies analyzed in ~60 seconds. Not theory.
ai_ml_infra.rare_combo: Most AI infrastructure engineers don't have the systems layer. I built the Rust MCP server underneath — zero-latency tool dispatch, no GC pauses, compliance-grade audit log.

protocol_engineer.depth: I work at the wire level. Most engineers consume protocols. I design them — transport, serialization format, dispatch, rate limiting, audit trail.
protocol_engineer.consensus: Built HotStuff 2-chain BFT consensus from scratch in Rust: view-change path, QC threshold, 8-test suite. EIP-1559 mempool with bounded eviction. Ethereum Engine API with 14-test suite.
protocol_engineer.rust: Rust is the right language for protocol work. No GC, no runtime surprises, no memory bugs. I've shipped a production protocol server and a BFT consensus implementation in it.
protocol_engineer.compliance: Built the full compliance stack on top of MCP: sliding-window rate limiter, PII detection, per-call audit log with output fingerprinting. Enterprise-ready.
